findings.evidence: `rg` shows `ContDiffAt.iteratedDerivWithin_Icc_eq_Ici` at `Integral.lean:56` already has arbitrary `{a x T t}`, while `ContDiffOn.integral_neg_derivWithin_Icc_eq_Ici` at `Integral.lean:83` hard-codes `{T}` and `Ioo 0 T`, and `IsCompletelyMonotone.integral_neg_deriv_Ici` at `Integral.lean:178` hard-codes the same `0..T` integral.
findings.fix: State the base lemma for arbitrary `x,T,a`, e.g. compare `∫ t in x..T, -iteratedDerivWithin 1 f (Icc x T) t` with `∫ t in x..T, -iteratedDerivWithin 1 f (Ici a) t` under `a ≤ x` and `x ≤ T`, then derive the current `0..T` lemmas as special cases if needed.
findings.issue: `ContDiffOn.integral_neg_derivWithin_Icc_eq_Ici` only proves the comparison for `∫ 0..T`, and the CM wrapper `IsCompletelyMonotone.integral_neg_deriv_Ici` at line 178 repeats the same zero-left specialization.

summary: The exported derivative-within transfer API is still specialized to the zero-left half-line case even though the adjacent pointwise theorem supports arbitrary intervals.
